patch.value: CONTEXT: pm captures local telemetry and flushes to a remote ingest endpoint; levels minimal/redacted/max, default redacted; first-run consent prompt (CI/non-TTY default-enabled). DECISION: events are persisted locally before send; sensitive data is stripped at capture (not at the network boundary); redacted is the permanent default and never auto-downgraded; max is opt-in. CONSEQUENCES: users keep control; even a compromised endpoint sees no secrets; minimal loses arg observability; analytics must handle sparse events. EVIDENCE: src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts, consent.ts; item pm-q9yt; decision-related pm-lnq3/pm-gusd.
ADR: Local-first telemetry with 'redacted' capture as the privileged default
Local-first telemetry with 'redacted' capture as the privileged default
ADR recorded and accepted; architectural decision documented as durable context on 2026-05-31.
